revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.
revision.text: #redirect[[en:Tsukahara Kofun (Shima)]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

'''塚原古墳'''（つかはらこふん）は三重県志摩市にある円墳。
[[埼玉県]][[行田市]]の[[稲荷山古墳]]から出土した[[画文帯環状乳神獣鏡]]（がもんたいかんじょうにゅうしんじゅうきょう）の兄弟鏡が出土している事から、[[雄略天皇]]の時代の[[古墳]]と見られている。
*出土品 :画文帯環状乳神獣鏡－1面、瑪瑙製曲玉3個、管玉22個、ガラス玉345個
現在、古墳はは消失。

*出土品 :画文帯環状乳神獣鏡－1面、瑪瑙製曲玉3個、管玉22個、ガラス玉345個
現在、古墳はは消失。
==関連項目== *[[日本の古墳一覧]] *[[稲荷山古墳]]－塚原古墳の同紋鏡が出土
[[category:古墳|つかはらこふん]]

revision.text:
'''塚原古墳'''（つかはらこふん）は三重県志摩市にあった円墳。
[[埼玉県]][[行田市]]の[[稲荷山古墳]]、[[群馬県]][[高崎市]][[八幡観音塚]]、[[宮崎県]][[新富町]][[山ノ坊古墳]]から出土した[[画文帯環状乳神獣鏡]]（がもんたいかんじょうにゅうしんじゅうきょう）の兄弟鏡が出土している。
*出土品 :画文帯環状乳神獣鏡1面、瑪瑙製曲玉3個、管玉22個、ガラス玉345個 :画文帯環状乳神獣鏡は現在、[[東京国立博物館]]に所蔵されている。
現在、古墳は消失。
== 関連項目 == *[[日本の古墳一覧]] *[[:Category:日本の古墳画像|日本の古墳画像一覧]] *[[泊古墳]]－志摩市の前方後円墳
[[category:古墳|つかはらこふん]] [[category:三重県の史跡・古墳|つかはらこふん]]

'''塚原古墳'''（つかはらこふん）は三重県志摩市にあった円墳。
[[埼玉県]][[行田市]]の[[稲荷山古墳]]、[[群馬県]][[高崎市]][[八幡観音塚]]、[[宮崎県]][[新富町]][[山ノ坊古墳]]から出土した[[画文帯環状乳神獣鏡]]（がもんたいかんじょうにゅうしんじゅうきょう）の兄弟鏡が出土している。
*出土品 :画文帯環状乳神獣鏡1面、瑪瑙製曲玉3個、管玉22個、ガラス玉345個 :画文帯環状乳神獣鏡は現在、[[東京国立博物館]]に所蔵されている。
[[明治]]～[[大正]]時代に削られ、現在古墳は消失した。直径20m、高さ3m程度であり、この付近の集落首長の墓であったと推察されている。

[[埼玉県]][[行田市]]の[[稲荷山古墳]]、[[群馬県]][[高崎市]][[八幡観音塚]]、[[宮崎県]][[新富町]][[山ノ坊古墳]]から出土した[[画文帯環状乳神獣鏡]]（がもんたいかんじょうにゅうしんじゅうきょう）の兄弟鏡が出土している。
*出土品 :画文帯環状乳神獣鏡1面、瑪瑙製曲玉3個、管玉22個、ガラス玉345個 :画文帯環状乳神獣鏡は現在、[[東京国立博物館]]に所蔵されている。
[[明治]]～[[大正]]時代に削られて古墳は消失した。直径20m、高さ3m程度であり、この付近の集落首長の墓であったと推察されている。
== 関連項目 == *[[:Category:日本の古墳画像|日本の古墳画像一覧]] *[[泊古墳]]－志摩市の前方後円墳

[[埼玉県]][[行田市]]の[[稲荷山古墳 (行田市)|稲荷山古墳]]、[[群馬県]][[高崎市]][[八幡観音塚古墳]]、[[宮崎県]][[新富町]][[山ノ坊古墳]]から出土した[[画文帯環状乳神獣鏡]]（がもんたいかんじょうにゅうしんじゅうきょう）の兄弟鏡が出土している。

== 文化財 == === 志摩市指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 画文帯環状乳神獣鏡（考古資料） - 志摩市歴史民俗資料館保管。1996年（平成8年）12月6日指定<ref>[https://www.city.shima.mie.jp/topics/1494470255038.html 志摩市の文化財]（志摩市ホームページ。）</ref>。

revision.comment: Created page with "[[File:Tsukahara Kofun (Shima) Artifacts.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Artifacts}}{{small|The Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror is a Shima City-designated cultural property. Displayed at the {{ill|Shima City Historical and Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}}.}}]] '''Tsukahara Kofun''' (塚原古墳) was a circular burial mound (円墳) located in {{ill|Shima City|ja|志摩市}}, {{ill|Daiocho|ja|大王町}}, Nakiri, Tsukahara, {{ill|Mie Prefecture..."
revision.text:
[[File:Tsukahara Kofun (Shima) Artifacts.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Artifacts}}{{small|The Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror is a Shima City-designated cultural property. Displayed at the {{ill|Shima City Historical and Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}}.}}]] '''Tsukahara Kofun''' (塚原古墳) was a circular burial mound (円墳) located in {{ill|Shima City|ja|志摩市}}, {{ill|Daiocho|ja|大王町}}, Nakiri, Tsukahara, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}.
A sibling mirror of the {{ill|Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror|ja|画文帯環状乳神獣鏡}}, similar to those unearthed from the {{ill|Inariyama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|稲荷山古墳 (行田市)}} in {{ill|Gyoda City|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, the {{ill|Yahata Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|八幡観音塚古墳}} in {{ill|Takasaki City|ja|高崎市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, and the {{ill|Yamanobo Kofun|ja|山ノ坊古墳}} in {{ill|Shintomi Town|ja|新富町}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|ja|宮崎県}}, was excavated here.
* **Excavated Artifacts** * Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror (1 piece) * Three magatama (curved beads) made of agate * 22 tubular beads * 345 glass beads
The Tsukahara Kofun was destroyed between the late Meiji and Taisho periods. It is estimated to have been about 20 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height, and it is believed to have served as the tomb of a local village chief.
== Cultural Properties == === Shima City Designated Cultural Properties === * **Tangible Cultural Properties** * Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror (archaeological artifact) - Stored at the {{ill|Shima City Historical and Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}}. Designated on December 6, 1996.<ref>[https://www.city.shima.mie.jp/topics/1494470255038.html Shima City Cultural Properties] (Shima City Official Website).</ref>

A sibling mirror of the {{ill|Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror|ja|画文帯環状乳神獣鏡}}, similar to those unearthed from the {{ill|Inariyama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|稲荷山古墳 (行田市)}} in {{ill|Gyoda City|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, the {{ill|Yahata Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|八幡観音塚古墳}} in {{ill|Takasaki City|ja|高崎市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, and the {{ill|Yamanobo Kofun|ja|山ノ坊古墳}} in {{ill|Shintomi Town|ja|新富町}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|ja|宮崎県}}, was excavated here.
* **Excavated Artifacts** * Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror (1 piece) * Three magatama (curved beads) made of agate * 22 tubular beads * 345 glass beads
The Tsukahara Kofun was destroyed between the late Meiji and Taisho periods. It is estimated to have been about 20 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height, and it is believed to have served as the tomb of a local village chief.
== Cultural Properties == === Shima City Designated Cultural Properties === * **Tangible Cultural Properties** * Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror (archaeological artifact) - Stored at the {{ill|Shima City Historical and Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}}. Designated on December 6, 1996.<ref>[https://www.city.shima.mie.jp/topics/1494470255038.html Shima City Cultural Properties] (Shima City Official Website).</ref>

revision.text:
[[File:Tsukahara Kofun (Shima) Artifacts.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Artifacts}}{{small|The Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror is a Shima City-designated cultural property. Displayed at the {{ill|Shima City Historical and Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}}.}}]] '''Tsukahara Kofun''' (塚原古墳) was a circular burial mound (円墳) located in {{ill|Shima City|ja|志摩市}}, {{ill|Daiocho|ja|大王町}}, Nakiri, Tsukahara, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}.
A sibling mirror of the {{ill|Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror|ja|画文帯環状乳神獣鏡}}, similar to those unearthed from the {{ill|Inariyama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|稲荷山古墳 (行田市)}} in {{ill|Gyoda City|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, the {{ill|Yahata Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|八幡観音塚古墳}} in {{ill|Takasaki City|ja|高崎市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, and the {{ill|Yamanobo Kofun|ja|山ノ坊古墳}} in {{ill|Shintomi Town|ja|新富町}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|ja|宮崎県}}, was excavated here.
* **Excavated Artifacts** * Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror (1 piece) * Three magatama (curved beads) made of agate * 22 tubular beads * 345 glass beads
The Tsukahara Kofun was destroyed between the late Meiji and Taisho periods. It is estimated to have been about 20 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height, and it is believed to have served as the tomb of a local village chief.
== Cultural Properties == === Shima City Designated Cultural Properties === * **Tangible Cultural Properties** * Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror (archaeological artifact) - Stored at the {{ill|Shima City Historical and Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}}. Designated on December 6, 1996.<ref>[https://www.city.shima.mie.jp/topics/1494470255038.html Shima City Cultural Properties] (Shima City Official Website).</ref>

A sibling mirror of the {{ill|Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror|ja|画文帯環状乳神獣鏡}}, similar to those unearthed from the {{ill|Inariyama Kofun|ja|稲荷山古墳 (行田市)}} in {{ill|Gyoda City|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, the {{ill|Hachiman Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|八幡観音塚古墳}} in {{ill|Takasaki City|ja|高崎市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, and the {{ill|Yamanobo Kofun|ja|山ノ坊古墳}} in {{ill|Shintomi Town|ja|新富町}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|ja|宮崎県}}, was excavated here.
* '''Excavated Artifacts''' ** Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror (1 piece) ** Three magatama (curved beads) made of agate ** 22 tubular beads ** 345 glass beads
The Tsukahara Kofun was destroyed between the late Meiji and Taisho periods. It is estimated to have been about 20 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height, and it is believed to have served as the tomb of a local village chief.
== Cultural Properties == === Shima City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' ** Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror (archaeological artifact) - Stored at the {{ill|Shima City Historical and Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}}. [[File:Tsukahara Kofun (Shima) Artifacts.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Artifacts}}{{small|The Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror is a Shima City-designated cultural property. Displayed at the {{ill|Shima City Historical and Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}}.}}]] '''Tsukahara Kofun''' (塚原古墳) was a circular burial mound (円墳) located in {{ill|Shima City|ja|志摩市}}, {{ill|Daiocho|ja|大王町}}, Nakiri, Tsukahara, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}.
A sibling mirror of the {{ill|Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror|ja|画文帯環状乳神獣鏡}}, similar to those unearthed from the {{ill|Inariyama Kofun|ja|稲荷山古墳 (行田市)}} in {{ill|Gyoda City|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, the {{ill|Hachiman Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|八幡観音塚古墳}} in {{ill|Takasaki City|ja|高崎市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, and the {{ill|Yamanobo Kofun|ja|山ノ坊古墳}} in {{ill|Shintomi Town|ja|新富町}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|ja|宮崎県}}, was excavated here.
* '''Excavated Artifacts''' ** Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror (1 piece) ** Three magatama (curved beads) made of agate ** 22 tubular beads ** 345 glass beads
The Tsukahara Kofun was destroyed between the late Meiji and Taisho periods. It is estimated to have been about 20 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height, and it is believed to have served as the tomb of a local village chief.
== Cultural Properties == === Shima City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' ** Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror (archaeological artifact) - Stored at the {{ill|Shima City Historical and Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}}. Designated on December 6, 1996.<ref>[https://www.city.shima.mie.jp/topics/1494470255038.html Shima City Cultural Properties] (Shima City Official Website).</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}} {{脚注ヘルプ}}

[[File:Tsukahara Kofun (Shima) Artifacts.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Artifacts}}{{small|The Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror is a Shima City-designated cultural property. Displayed at the {{ill|Shima City Historical and Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}}.}}]] '''Tsukahara Kofun''' (塚原古墳) was a circular burial mound (円墳) located in {{ill|Shima City|ja|志摩市}}, {{ill|Daiocho|ja|大王町}}, Nakiri, Tsukahara, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}.
A sibling mirror of the {{ill|Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror|ja|画文帯環状乳神獣鏡}}, similar to those unearthed from the {{ill|Inariyama Kofun|ja|稲荷山古墳 (行田市)}} in {{ill|Gyoda City|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, the {{ill|Hachiman Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|八幡観音塚古墳}} in {{ill|Takasaki City|ja|高崎市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, and the {{ill|Yamanobo Kofun|ja|山ノ坊古墳}} in {{ill|Shintomi Town|ja|新富町}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|ja|宮崎県}}, was excavated here.
* '''Excavated Artifacts''' ** Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror (1 piece) ** Three magatama (curved beads) made of agate ** 22 tubular beads ** 345 glass beads
The Tsukahara Kofun was destroyed between the late Meiji and Taisho periods. It is estimated to have been about 20 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height, and it is believed to have served as the tomb of a local village chief.
== Cultural Properties == === Shima City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' ** Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror (archaeological artifact) - Stored at the {{ill|Shima City Historical and Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}}. Designated on December 6, 1996.<ref>[https://www.city.shima.mie.jp/topics/1494470255038.html Shima City Cultural Properties] (Shima City Official Website).</ref>

A sibling mirror of the {{ill|Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror|ja|画文帯環状乳神獣鏡}}, similar to those unearthed from the {{ill|Inariyama Kofun|ja|稲荷山古墳 (行田市)}} in {{ill|Gyoda City|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, the {{ill|Hachiman Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|八幡観音塚古墳}} in {{ill|Takasaki City|ja|高崎市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, and the {{ill|Yamanobo Kofun|ja|山ノ坊古墳}} in {{ill|Shintomi Town|ja|新富町}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|ja|宮崎県}}, was excavated here.
* '''Excavated Artifacts''' ** Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror (1 piece) ** Three magatama (curved beads) made of agate ** 22 tubular beads ** 345 glass beads
The Tsukahara Kofun was destroyed between the late Meiji and Taisho periods. It is estimated to have been about 20 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height, and it is believed to have served as the tomb of a local village chief.

Displayed at the {{ill|Shima City Historical and Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}}.}}]] '''Tsukahara Kofun''' (塚原古墳) was a circular burial mound (円墳) located in {{ill|Shima City|ja|志摩市}}, {{ill|Daiocho|ja|大王町}}, Nakiri, Tsukahara, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}.
A sibling mirror of the {{ill|Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror|ja|画文帯環状乳神獣鏡}}, similar to those unearthed from the {{ill|Inariyama Kofun|ja|稲荷山古墳 (行田市)}} in {{ill|Gyoda City|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, the {{ill|Hachiman Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|八幡観音塚古墳}} in {{ill|Takasaki City|ja|高崎市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, and the {{ill|Yamanobo Kofun|ja|山ノ坊古墳}} in {{ill|Shintomi Town|ja|新富町}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|ja|宮崎県}}, was excavated here.
* '''Excavated Artifacts''' ** Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror (1 piece) ** Three magatama (curved beads) made of agate ** 22 tubular beads ** 345 glass beads
The Tsukahara Kofun was destroyed between the late Meiji and Taisho periods. It is estimated to have been about 20 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height, and it is believed to have served as the tomb of a local village chief.
== Cultural Properties == === Shima City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' ** Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror (archaeological artifact) - Stored at the {{ill|Shima City Historical and Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}}. Designated on December 6, 1996.<ref>[https://www.city.shima.mie.jp/topics/1494470255038.html Shima City Cultural Properties] (Shima City Official Website).</ref>

[[File:Tsukahara Kofun (Shima) Artifacts.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Artifacts}}{{small|The Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror is a Shima City-designated cultural property. Displayed at the {{ill|Shima City Historical and Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}}.}}]] '''Tsukahara Kofun''' (塚原古墳) was a circular burial mound (円墳) located in {{ill|Shima City|ja|志摩市}}, {{ill|Daiocho|ja|大王町}}, Nakiri, Tsukahara, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}.
A sibling mirror of the {{ill|Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror|ja|画文帯環状乳神獣鏡}}, similar to those unearthed from the {{ill|Inariyama Kofun|ja|稲荷山古墳 (行田市)}} in {{ill|Gyoda City|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, the {{ill|Hachiman Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|八幡観音塚古墳}} in {{ill|Takasaki City|ja|高崎市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, and the {{ill|Yamanobo Kofun|ja|山ノ坊古墳}} in {{ill|Shintomi Town|ja|新富町}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|ja|宮崎県}}, was excavated here.
* '''Excavated Artifacts''' ** Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror (1 piece) ** Three magatama (curved beads) made of agate ** 22 tubular beads ** 345 glass beads
The Tsukahara Kofun was destroyed between the late Meiji and Taisho periods. It is estimated to have been about 20 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height, and it is believed to have served as the tomb of a local village chief.
== Cultural Properties == === Shima City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' ** Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror (archaeological artifact) - Stored at the {{ill|Shima City Historical and Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}}. Designated on December 6, 1996.<ref>[https://www.city.shima.mie.jp/topics/1494470255038.html Shima City Cultural Properties] (Shima City Official Website).</ref>

[[File:Tsukahara Kofun (Shima) Artifacts.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Artifacts}}{{small|The Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror is a Shima City-designated cultural property. Displayed at the {{ill|Shima City Historical and Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}}.}}]] '''Tsukahara Kofun''' (塚原古墳) was a circular burial mound (円墳) located in {{ill|Shima City|ja|志摩市}}, {{ill|Daiocho|ja|大王町}}, Nakiri, Tsukahara, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}.
A sibling mirror of the {{ill|Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror|ja|画文帯環状乳神獣鏡}}, similar to those unearthed from the {{ill|Inariyama Kofun|ja|稲荷山古墳 (行田市)}} in {{ill|Gyoda City|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, the {{ill|Hachiman Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|八幡観音塚古墳}} in {{ill|Takasaki City|ja|高崎市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, and the {{ill|Yamanobo Kofun|ja|山ノ坊古墳}} in {{ill|Shintomi Town|ja|新富町}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|ja|宮崎県}}, was excavated here.
* '''Excavated Artifacts''' ** Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror (1 piece) ** Three magatama (curved beads) made of agate ** 22 tubular beads ** 345 glass beads
The Tsukahara Kofun was destroyed between the late Meiji and Taisho periods. It is estimated to have been about 20 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height, and it is believed to have served as the tomb of a local village chief.
== Cultural Properties == === Shima City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' ** Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror (archaeological artifact) - Stored at the {{ill|Shima City Historical and Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}}. Designated on December 6, 1996.<ref>[https://www.city.shima.mie.jp/topics/1494470255038.html Shima City Cultural Properties] (Shima City Official Website).</ref>

Displayed at the {{ill|Shima City Historical and Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}}.}}]] '''Tsukahara Kofun''' (塚原古墳) was a circular burial mound (円墳) located in {{ill|Shima City|ja|志摩市}}, {{ill|Daiocho|ja|大王町}}, Nakiri, Tsukahara, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}.
A sibling mirror of the {{ill|Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror|ja|画文帯環状乳神獣鏡}}, similar to those unearthed from the {{ill|Inariyama Kofun|ja|稲荷山古墳 (行田市)}} in {{ill|Gyoda City|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, the {{ill|Hachiman Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|八幡観音塚古墳}} in {{ill|Takasaki City|ja|高崎市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, and the {{ill|Yamanobo Kofun|ja|山ノ坊古墳}} in {{ill|Shintomi Town|ja|新富町}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|ja|宮崎県}}, was excavated here.
* '''Excavated Artifacts''' ** Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror (1 piece) ** Three magatama (curved beads) made of agate ** 22 tubular beads ** 345 glass beads
The Tsukahara Kofun was destroyed between the late Meiji and Taisho periods. It is estimated to have been about 20 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height, and it is believed to have served as the tomb of a local village chief.
== Cultural Properties == === Shima City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' ** Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror (archaeological artifact) - Stored at the {{ill|Shima City Historical and Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}}. [[File:Tsukahara Kofun (Shima) Artifacts.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Artifacts}}{{small|The Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror is a Shima City-designated cultural property. Displayed at the {{ill|Shima City Historical and Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}}.}}]] '''Tsukahara Kofun''' (塚原古墳) was a circular burial mound (円墳) located in {{ill|Shima City|ja|志摩市}}, {{ill|Daiocho|ja|大王町}}, Nakiri, Tsukahara, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}.
A sibling mirror of the {{ill|Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror|ja|画文帯環状乳神獣鏡}}, similar to those unearthed from the {{ill|Inariyama Kofun|ja|稲荷山古墳 (行田市)}} in {{ill|Gyoda City|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, the {{ill|Hachiman Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|八幡観音塚古墳}} in {{ill|Takasaki City|ja|高崎市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, and the {{ill|Yamanobo Kofun|ja|山ノ坊古墳}} in {{ill|Shintomi Town|ja|新富町}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|ja|宮崎県}}, was excavated here.
* '''Excavated Artifacts''' ** Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror (1 piece) ** Three magatama (curved beads) made of agate ** 22 tubular beads ** 345 glass beads
The Tsukahara Kofun was destroyed between the late Meiji and Taisho periods. It is estimated to have been about 20 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height, and it is believed to have served as the tomb of a local village chief.
== Cultural Properties == === Shima City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' ** Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror (archaeological artifact) - Stored at the {{ill|Shima City Historical and Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}}. Designated on December 6, 1996.<ref>[https://www.city.shima.mie.jp/topics/1494470255038.html Shima City Cultural Properties] (Shima City Official Website).</ref>

[[File:Tsukahara Kofun (Shima) Artifacts.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Artifacts}}{{small|The Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror is a Shima City-designated cultural property. Displayed at the {{ill|Shima City Historical and Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}}.}}]] '''Tsukahara Kofun''' (塚原古墳) was a circular Kofun (円墳) located in {{ill|Shima City|ja|志摩市}}, {{ill|Daiocho|ja|大王町}}, Nakiri, Tsukahara, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}.
A sibling mirror of the {{ill|Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror|ja|画文帯環状乳神獣鏡}}, similar to those unearthed from the {{ill|Inariyama Kofun|ja|稲荷山古墳 (行田市)}} in {{ill|Gyoda City|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, the {{ill|Hachiman Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|八幡観音塚古墳}} in {{ill|Takasaki City|ja|高崎市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, and the {{ill|Yamanobo Kofun|ja|山ノ坊古墳}} in {{ill|Shintomi Town|ja|新富町}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|ja|宮崎県}}, was excavated here.
* '''Excavated Artifacts''' ** Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror (1 piece) ** Three magatama (curved beads) made of agate ** 22 tubular beads ** 345 glass beads
The Tsukahara Kofun was destroyed between the late Meiji and Taisho periods. It is estimated to have been about 20 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height, and it is believed to have served as the tomb of a local village chief.
== Cultural Properties == === Shima City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' ** Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror (archaeological artifact) - Stored at the {{ill|Shima City Historical and Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}}. Designated on December 6, 1996.<ref>[https://www.city.shima.mie.jp/topics/1494470255038.html Shima City Cultural Properties] (Shima City Official Website).</ref>

revision.text:
[[File:Tsukahara Kofun (Shima) Artifacts.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Artifacts}}{{small|The Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror is a Shima City-designated cultural property. Displayed at the {{ill|Shima City Historical and Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}}.}}]] '''Tsukahara Kofun''' (塚原古墳) was a circular Kofun (円墳) located in {{ill|Shima City|ja|志摩市}}, {{ill|Daiocho|ja|大王町}}, Nakiri, Tsukahara, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}.
A sibling mirror of the {{ill|Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror|ja|画文帯環状乳神獣鏡}}, similar to those unearthed from the {{ill|Inariyama Kofun|ja|稲荷山古墳 (行田市)}} in {{ill|Gyoda City|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, the {{ill|Hachiman Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|八幡観音塚古墳}} in {{ill|Takasaki City|ja|高崎市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, and the {{ill|Yamanobo Kofun|ja|山ノ坊古墳}} in {{ill|Shintomi Town|ja|新富町}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|ja|宮崎県}}, was excavated here.
* '''Excavated Artifacts''' ** Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror (1 piece) ** Three magatama (curved beads) made of agate ** 22 tubular beads ** 345 glass beads
The Tsukahara Kofun was destroyed between the late Meiji and Taisho periods. It is estimated to have been about 20 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height, and it is believed to have served as the tomb of a local village chief.
== Cultural Properties == === Shima City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' ** Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror (archaeological artifact) - Stored at the {{ill|Shima City Historical and Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}}. Designated on December 6, 1996.<ref>[https://www.city.shima.mie.jp/topics/1494470255038.html Shima City Cultural Properties] (Shima City Official Website).</ref>

A sibling mirror of the {{ill|Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror|ja|画文帯環状乳神獣鏡}}, similar to those unearthed from the {{ill|Inariyama Kofun|ja|稲荷山古墳 (行田市)}} in {{ill|Gyoda City|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, the {{ill|Hachiman Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|八幡観音塚古墳}} in {{ill|Takasaki City|ja|高崎市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, and the {{ill|Yamanobo Kofun|ja|山ノ坊古墳}} in {{ill|Shintomi Town|ja|新富町}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|ja|宮崎県}}, was excavated here.
* '''Excavated Artifacts''' ** Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror (1 piece) ** Three magatama (curved beads) made of agate ** 22 tubular beads ** 345 glass beads
The Tsukahara Kofun was destroyed between the late Meiji and Taisho periods. It is estimated to have been about 20 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height, and it is believed to have served as the tomb of a local village chief.

A sibling mirror of the {{ill|Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror|ja|画文帯環状乳神獣鏡|12=draft}}, similar to those unearthed from the {{ill|Inariyama Kofun|ja|稲荷山古墳 (行田市)|12=draft}} in {{ill|Gyoda City|ja|行田市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Hachiman Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|八幡観音塚古墳|12=draft}} in {{ill|Takasaki City|ja|高崎市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|12=draft}}, and the {{ill|Yamanobo Kofun|ja|山ノ坊古墳|12=draft}} in {{ill|Shintomi Town|ja|新富町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|ja|宮崎県|12=draft}}, was excavated here.
* '''Excavated Artifacts''' ** Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror (1 piece) ** Three magatama (curved beads) made of agate ** 22 tubular beads ** 345 glass beads
The Tsukahara Kofun was destroyed between the late Meiji and Taisho periods. It is estimated to have been about 20 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height, and it is believed to have served as the tomb of a local village chief.
== Cultural Properties == === Shima City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' ** Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror (archaeological artifact) - Stored at the {{ill|Shima City Historical and Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館|12=draft}}. Designated on December 6, 1996.<ref>[https://www.city.shima.mie.jp/topics/1494470255038.html Shima City Cultural Properties] (Shima City Official Website).</ref>

[[File:Tsukahara Kofun (Shima) Artifacts.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|burial goods}}{{small|The Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror is a Shima City-designated cultural property. Displayed at the {{ill|Shima City Historical and Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館|12=draft}}.}}]] '''Tsukahara Kofun''' (塚原古墳) was a circular Kofun (円墳) located in {{ill|Shima City|ja|志摩市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Daiocho|ja|大王町|12=draft}}, Nakiri, Tsukahara, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}.
A sibling mirror of the {{ill|Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror|ja|画文帯環状乳神獣鏡|12=draft}}, similar to those unearthed from the {{ill|Inariyama Kofun|ja|稲荷山古墳 (行田市)|12=draft}} in {{ill|Gyoda City|ja|行田市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Hachiman Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|八幡観音塚古墳|12=draft}} in {{ill|Takasaki City|ja|高崎市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|12=draft}}, and the {{ill|Yamanobo Kofun|ja|山ノ坊古墳|12=draft}} in {{ill|Shintomi Town|ja|新富町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|ja|宮崎県|12=draft}}, was excavated here.
* '''Excavated Artifacts''' ** Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror (1 piece) ** Three magatama (curved beads) made of agate ** 22 tubular beads ** 345 glass beads
The Tsukahara Kofun was destroyed between the late Meiji and Taisho periods. It is estimated to have been about 20 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height, and it is believed to have served as the tomb of a local village chief.
== Cultural Properties == === Shima City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' ** Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror (archaeological artifact) - Stored at the {{ill|Shima City Historical and Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館|12=draft}}. Designated on December 6, 1996.<ref>[https://www.city.shima.mie.jp/topics/1494470255038.html Shima City Cultural Properties] (Shima City Official Website).</ref>

revision.comment: Bot: single‑shot reformat + ill→|12=draft + draft categories + iws + DEFAULTSORT + move HTML comments + meta‑cat check
28 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:塚原古墳 (志摩市) up to rev 75052705
revision.text:
[[ファイル:塚原古墳 (志摩市) 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|出土品}}{{small|画文帯環状乳神獣鏡は志摩市指定文化財。[[志摩市歴史民俗資料館]]展示。}}]] '''塚原古墳'''（つかはらこふん）は[[三重県]][[志摩市]][[大王町]]波切字塚原にあった円墳。
[[埼玉県]][[行田市]]の[[稲荷山古墳 (行田市)|稲荷山古墳]]、[[群馬県]][[高崎市]][[八幡観音塚古墳]]、[[宮崎県]][[新富町]][[山ノ坊古墳]]から出土した[[画文帯環状乳神獣鏡]]（がもんたいかんじょうにゅうしんじゅうきょう）の兄弟鏡が出土している。
* 出土品 *:画文帯環状乳[[神獣鏡]]1面、瑪瑙製曲玉3個、管玉22個、ガラス玉345個
[[明治]] - [[大正]]時代に削られて古墳は消失した。直径20m、高さ3m程度であり、この付近の集落首長の墓であったと推察されている。
== 文化財 == === 志摩市指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 画文帯環状乳神獣鏡（考古資料） - 志摩市歴史民俗資料館保管。1996年（平成8年）12月6日指定<ref>[https://www.city.shima.mie.jp/topics/1494470255038.html 志摩市の文化財]（志摩市ホームページ。）</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:つかはらこふん しまし}} [[Category:三重県の古墳]] [[Category:志摩市の歴史]] [[Category:円墳]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:塚原古墳 (志摩市)]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Shima|qid=Q338009}}|architectural_styles={{ill|circular kofun|qid=Q11394747}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11428409]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:06, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Tsukahara Kofun (Shima) Artifacts.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|burial goods}}{{small|The Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror is a Shima City-designated cultural property. Displayed at the {{ill|Shima City Historical and Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima City Historical and Folklore Museum|qq=|1=Shima City Museum of History and Folklore|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11490924}}.}}]] '''Tsukahara Kofun''' (塚原古墳) was a circular Kofun (円墳) located in [[:en:Shima, Mie|Shima City]], [[:en:Daiō, Mie|Daiocho]], Nakiri, Tsukahara, [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]].
A sibling mirror of the {{ill|Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror|ja|画文帯環状乳神獣鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, similar to those unearthed from the [[:en:Inariyama Kofun|Inariyama Kofun]] in [[:en:Gyōda|Gyoda City]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], the [[:en:Hachiman Kannonzuka Kofun|Hachiman Kannonzuka Kofun]] in [[:en:Takasaki|Takasaki City]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], and the {{ill|Yamanobo Kofun|ja|山ノ坊古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanobo Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Shintomi, Miyazaki|Shintomi Town]], [[:en:Miyazaki Prefecture|Miyazaki Prefecture]], was excavated here.
* '''Excavated Artifacts''' ** Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror (1 piece) ** Three magatama (curved beads) made of agate ** 22 tubular beads ** 345 glass beads
The Tsukahara Kofun was destroyed between the late Meiji and Taisho periods. It is estimated to have been about 20 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height, and it is believed to have served as the tomb of a local village chief.

A sibling mirror of the {{ill|Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror|ja|画文帯環状乳神獣鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, similar to those unearthed from the [[:en:Inariyama Kofun|Inariyama Kofun]] in [[:en:Gyōda|Gyoda City]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], the [[:en:Hachiman Kannonzuka Kofun|Hachiman Kannonzuka Kofun]] in [[:en:Takasaki|Takasaki City]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], and the {{ill|Yamanobo Kofun|ja|山ノ坊古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanobo Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Shintomi, Miyazaki|Shintomi Town]], [[:en:Miyazaki Prefecture|Miyazaki Prefecture]], was excavated here.
* '''Excavated Artifacts''' ** Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror (1 piece) ** Three magatama (curved beads) made of agate ** 22 tubular beads ** 345 glass beads
The Tsukahara Kofun was destroyed between the late Meiji and Taisho periods. It is estimated to have been about 20 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height, and it is believed to have served as the tomb of a local village chief.
== Cultural Properties == === Shima City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' ** Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror (archaeological artifact) - Stored at the {{ill|Shima City Historical and Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima City Historical and Folklore Museum|1=Shima City Museum of History and Folklore|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11490924}}. Designated on December 6, 1996.<ref>[https://www.city.shima.mie.jp/topics/1494470255038.html Shima City Cultural Properties] (Shima City Official Website).</ref>

A sibling mirror of the {{ill|Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror|ja|画文帯環状乳神獣鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, similar to those unearthed from the [[:en:Inariyama Kofun|Inariyama Kofun]] in [[:en:Gyōda|Gyoda City]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], the [[:en:Hachiman Kannonzuka Kofun|Hachiman Kannonzuka Kofun]] in [[:en:Takasaki|Takasaki City]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], and the {{ill|Yamanobo Kofun|ja|山ノ坊古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanobo Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Shintomi, Miyazaki|Shintomi Town]], [[:en:Miyazaki Prefecture|Miyazaki Prefecture]], was excavated here.
* '''Excavated Artifacts''' ** Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror (1 piece) ** Three magatama (curved beads) made of agate ** 22 tubular beads ** 345 glass beads
The Tsukahara Kofun was destroyed between the late Meiji and Taisho periods. It is estimated to have been about 20 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height, and it is believed to have served as the tomb of a local village chief.
== Cultural Properties == === Shima City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' ** Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror (archaeological artifact) - Stored at the {{ill|Shima City Historical and Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima City Historical and Folklore Museum|1=Shima City Museum of History and Folklore|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11490924}}. Designated on December 6, 1996.<ref>[https://www.city.shima.mie.jp/topics/1494470255038.html Shima City Cultural Properties] (Shima City Official Website).</ref>

A sibling mirror of the {{ill|Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror|ja|画文帯環状乳神獣鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, similar to those unearthed from the [[:en:Inariyama Kofun|Inariyama Kofun]] in [[:en:Gyōda|Gyoda City]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], the [[:en:Hachiman Kannonzuka Kofun|Hachiman Kannonzuka Kofun]] in [[:en:Takasaki|Takasaki City]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], and the {{ill|Yamanobo Kofun|ja|山ノ坊古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanobo Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Shintomi, Miyazaki|Shintomi Town]], [[:en:Miyazaki Prefecture|Miyazaki Prefecture]], was excavated here.
* '''Excavated Artifacts''' ** Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror (1 piece) ** Three magatama (curved beads) made of agate ** 22 tubular beads ** 345 glass beads
The Tsukahara Kofun was destroyed between the late Meiji and Taisho periods. It is estimated to have been about 20 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height, and it is believed to have served as the tomb of a local village chief.
== Cultural Properties == === Shima City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' ** Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror (archaeological artifact) - Stored at the {{ill|Shima City Historical and Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima City Historical and Folklore Museum|1=Shima City Museum of History and Folklore|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11490924}}. Designated on December 6, 1996.<ref>[https://www.city.shima.mie.jp/topics/1494470255038.html Shima City Cultural Properties] (Shima City Official Website).</ref>

A sibling mirror of the {{ill|Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror|ja|画文帯環状乳神獣鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, similar to those unearthed from the [[:en:Inariyama Kofun|Inariyama Kofun]] in [[:en:Gyōda|Gyoda City]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], the [[:en:Hachiman Kannonzuka Kofun|Hachiman Kannonzuka Kofun]] in [[:en:Takasaki|Takasaki City]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], and the {{ill|Yamanobo Kofun|ja|山ノ坊古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanobo Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Shintomi, Miyazaki|Shintomi Town]], [[:en:Miyazaki Prefecture|Miyazaki Prefecture]], was excavated here.
* '''Excavated Artifacts''' ** Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror (1 piece) ** Three magatama (curved beads) made of agate ** 22 tubular beads ** 345 glass beads
The Tsukahara Kofun was destroyed between the late Meiji and Taisho periods. It is estimated to have been about 20 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height, and it is believed to have served as the tomb of a local village chief.
== Cultural Properties == === Shima City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' ** Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror (archaeological artifact) - Stored at the {{ill|Shima City Historical and Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima City Historical and Folklore Museum|1=Shima City Museum of History and Folklore|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11490924}}. Designated on December 6, 1996.<ref>[https://www.city.shima.mie.jp/topics/1494470255038.html Shima City Cultural Properties] (Shima City Official Website).</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:塚原古墳 (志摩市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:塚原古墳 (志摩市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:塚原古墳 (志摩市)]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Shima|WD=Q338009|1=Shima, Mie}}|architectural_styles={{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747|1=Round Kofun}}}} [[File:Tsukahara Kofun (Shima) Artifacts.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|burial goods}}{{small|The Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror is a Shima City-designated cultural property. Displayed at the {{ill|Shima City Historical and Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima City Historical and Folklore Museum|1=Shima Historical Folklore Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11490924}}.}}]] '''Tsukahara Kofun''' (塚原古墳) was a circular Kofun (円墳) located in {{ill|Shima, Mie|en|Shima, Mie|WD=Q338009|lt=Shima City|1=Shima, Mie}}, {{ill|Daiō, Mie|en|Daiō, Mie|WD=Q5209858|lt=Daiocho|1=Daiō, Mie}}, Nakiri, Tsukahara, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}.
A sibling mirror of the {{ill|Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror|ja|画文帯環状乳神獣鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, similar to those unearthed from the {{ill|Inariyama Kofun|en|Inariyama Kofun|WD=Q3198272|lt=Inariyama Kofun|1=Inariyama Kofun}} in {{ill|Gyōda|en|Gyōda|WD=Q860659|lt=Gyoda City|1=Gyōda}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}}, the {{ill|Hachiman Kannonzuka Kofun|en|Hachiman Kannonzuka Kofun|WD=Q17222413|lt=Hachiman Kannonzuka Kofun|1=Hachiman Kannonzuka Kofun}} in {{ill|Takasaki|en|Takasaki|WD=Q336438|lt=Takasaki City|1=Takasaki}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}}, and the {{ill|Yamanobo Kofun|ja|山ノ坊古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanobo Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Shintomi, Miyazaki|en|Shintomi, Miyazaki|WD=Q1206753|lt=Shintomi Town|1=Shintomi, Miyazaki}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|en|Miyazaki Prefecture|WD=Q130300|lt=Miyazaki Prefecture|1=Miyazaki Prefecture}}, was excavated here.
* '''Excavated Artifacts''' ** Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror (1 piece) ** Three magatama (curved beads) made of agate ** 22 tubular beads ** 345 glass beads
The Tsukahara Kofun was destroyed between the late Meiji and Taisho periods. It is estimated to have been about 20 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height, and it is believed to have served as the tomb of a local village chief.
== Cultural Properties == === Shima City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' ** Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror (archaeological artifact) - Stored at the {{ill|Shima City Historical and Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima City Historical and Folklore Museum|1=Shima Historical Folklore Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11490924}}. Designated on December 6, 1996.<ref>[https://www.city.shima.mie.jp/topics/1494470255038.html Shima City Cultural Properties] (Shima City Official Website).</ref>

* '''Excavated Artifacts''' ** Gamontai Kanshōnyū Divine Beast Mirror (1 piece) ** Three magatama (curved beads) made of agate ** 22 tubular beads ** 345 glass beads
The Tsukahara Kofun was destroyed between the late Meiji and Taisho periods. It is estimated to have been about 20 meters in diameter and 3 meters in height, and it is believed to have served as the tomb of a local village chief.
